verdict_reasoning: VEGFA is a secreted, disulfide-linked homodimeric growth factor of the PDGF/VEGF family that signals through TM receptors (VEGFR1/2/3, NRP1/2) on endothelial cells. Anti-VEGFA antibodies (bevacizumab, ranibizumab) target the circulating/soluble ligand, not a surface pool on producer cells. Walking the contextual buckets: no documented cancer/cell-death/activation outer-leaflet ecto-pool of VEGFA itself; no narrow germline/developmental surface display with its own anchor; not a lysosomal TM protein; no PM minority pool — heparin/HSPG binding is reversible ECM/matrix association in equilibrium with the soluble pool, failing the wash test; no covalent/wash-resistant TM-partner tethering (binding to VEGFR is reversible recruitment, recruiter is the receptor). No TM isoform exists.
